content.text: I'll help you swap the positions of the Building_Key and AreaID dropdown fields. Let me first read the file to see the current structure.

content.content:
1→@using NonFactors.Mvc.Grid; 2→@using System.Data; 3→@using System.Data.SqlClient; 4→ 5→@model Firstronic.Core.Entities.App.First.tbl_PD_idt_stations_FULL 6→ 7→@{ 8→ bool isNew = !(Model != null && !String.IsNullOrEmpty(Model.StationName)); 9→} 10→ 11→@{ 12→ //Dropdowns Here Instead controller... 13→ var AreaList = new List(); 14→ AreaList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetAreasList(null, ""); 15→ Model.AreaList = AreaList; 16→ 17→ var IsActiveList = new List(); 18→ IsActiveList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 19→ Model.IsActiveList = IsActiveList; 20→ 21→ var LockStatusList = new List(); 22→ LockStatusList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 23→ Model.LockStatusList = LockStatusList; 24→ 25→ var BeFirstActiveList = new List(); 26→ BeFirstActiveList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 27→ Model.BeFirstActiveList = BeFirstActiveList; 39→ Model.LockMachinesList = LockMachinesList; 40→ 41→ var Allow_Plex_TransactionsList = new List(); 42→ Allow_Plex_TransactionsList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 43→ Model.Allow_Plex_TransactionsList = Allow_Plex_TransactionsList; 44→ 45→ 46→ // GET Other Values... 47→ 48→ var AutoScanList = new List(); 49→ AutoScanList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 50→ Model.AutoScanList = AutoScanList; 51→ 52→ Model.AutoScan = false; 53→ 54→ SqlParameter[] parameters = new SqlParameter[]{ 55→ new SqlParameter("@Id",SqlDbType.Int){Value = Model.StationID ?? -1}, 56→ new SqlParameter("@AreaID",SqlDbType.Int){Value = Model.AreaID ?? -1}, 57→ }; 58→ 59→ //Model.AutoScan = Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetBooleanFromQuery("Select CAST(CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS BIT) FROM PD_tbl_station_autoscan WHERE stationid=@Id", parameters); 60→ 61→ 62→ var ValidationToRunList = new List(); 63→ ValidationToRunList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 64→ Model.ValidationToRunList = ValidationToRunList; 65→ 66→ Model.ValidationToRun = false; 67→ 68→ Model.ValidationToRun = Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetBooleanFromQuery("Select CAST(CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS BIT) FROM PD_tbl_stations_req_val WHERE stationid=@Id", parameters); 69→ 70→ 71→ var Shipping_ValidateQtyList = new List(); 72→ Shipping_ValidateQtyList = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 73→ Model.Shipping_ValidateQtyList = Shipping_ValidateQtyList; 74→ 75→ Model.Shipping_ValidateQty = false; 76→ 77→ Model.Shipping_ValidateQty = Firstronic.Framework.Queries.GetBooleanFromQuery("Select CAST(CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS BIT) FROM Part_Operation_Shipping_Validate_BoxQty WHERE stationid=@Id", parameters); 78→ 79→ var Allow_LoadMaterial_List = new List(); 80→ Allow_LoadMaterial_List = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 81→ var Custom_Labels_List = new List(); 82→ Custom_Labels_List = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 83→ var Allow_Plex_Transactions_2_List = new List(); 84→ Allow_Plex_Transactions_2_List = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); 85→ var AbleToLock_List = new List(); 86→ AbleToLock_List = Firstronic.Framework.Functions.Dropdowns.GetBooleanList(null); content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully swapped the positions of the Building_Key and AreaID dropdown fields in the Station form. 

## Summary

**File Modified:** `S:\01_Repos\BeFirst_Portal\firstronic\Areas\Manage\Views\Station\_CreateOrUpdate.cshtml`

**Changes Made:**
- **Building_Key dropdown** moved from Row 3, Column 1 to Row 2, Column 3 (after ALTERNATIVE_NAME_2)
- **AreaID dropdown** moved from Row 2, Column 3 to Row 3, Column 1 (before IsActive)

**Result:**
This creates better visual balance in the form layout:
- Row 1: Facility_Name, StationName, Alias
- Row 2: ALTERNATIVE_NAME_1, ALTERNATIVE_NAME_2, **Building_Key** (with `@id="ddlBuild"`)
- Row 3: **AreaID**, IsActive, LockStatus

The `@id` attributes remain intact (`ddlBuild` for Building_Key), ensuring the JavaScript functionality that depends on `#ddlBuild` (line 521) continues to work correctly.
